title: Estimate race-specific pit-loss and warm-up economics
summary: F1 pit strategy is a cost-benefit decision: pit-loss, fresh-tyre gain, out-lap warm-up, and current degradation jointly decide whether stopping now pays.
rationale: F1 pit strategy is a cost-benefit decision: pit-loss, fresh-tyre gain, out-lap warm-up, and current degradation jointly decide whether stopping now pays. Current solutions mostly use tyre-window feasibility, not the local economic break-even.
implementation_hint: From PitStop rows, estimate Race-Year median in-lap/out-lap lap-time excess, post-stop warm-up penalty for laps 1-3, and current-stint degradation slope. Add utility features such as expected_next_3_lap_gain_minus_pit_loss and break_even_laps_to_recover_stop.
expected_effect: Improves ranking around marginal stop windows, especially one-stop versus two-stop decisions.
risk: LapTime outliers from cautions/red flags can corrupt estimates; use robust medians and fallback to Race-level or global priors when data is sparse.
